steps.title: Identify the tense context
steps.content: The passage describes a recurring, nightly event using present-tense verbs: "is bathed" and "is the Árbol de la Sidra." The phrase "Each night" signals a habitual action happening now. The blank must match this present-tense, habitual pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'will be illuminating': Future progressive tense. The event is happening now every night, not at some future time. ✗
  • Choice B — 'illuminates': Simple present tense. Matches the present-tense verbs "is bathed" and "is" used throughout the passage to describe what happens each night. ✓
  • Choice C — 'would illuminate': The modal "would" indicates a typical behavior in the past, not a present recurring action. ✗
  • Choice D — 'illuminated': Past tense. The description is of what happens each night currently, not what happened in the past. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
